vocabulary.usage_note: Time adverb for the current day: 'Heute gehe ich ins Kino.' Always used without an article.
Used before clock times: 'um drei Uhr' (at three o'clock). Also used in 'um … Uhr' expressions. Takes accusative.

vocabulary.english: on (with days and parts of the day)
vocabulary.usage_note: Contraction of 'an + dem'. Used with days of the week (am Montag) and parts of the day (am Abend, am Nachmittag). Essential for scheduling language.
Used for going to venues in Lektion 8: 'in die Disco', 'ins Kino' (in + das). With location: 'im Café' (in + dem). Movement → accusative; location → dative.
Used in clock times: 'zehn nach drei' (ten past three). Also used with places: 'nach Wien fahren' (go to Vienna).
Used in clock times: 'zehn vor vier' (ten to four). With time expressions it takes dative: 'vor dem Essen' (before the meal).

vocabulary.english: half past (but expressed as half BEFORE the next hour)
vocabulary.usage_note: CAUTION: 'halb zehn' means 9:30 (half before ten), NOT 10:30. English 'half ten' = 10:30; German 'halb zehn' = 9:30. Spanish speakers: 'son las nueve y media' = 'halb zehn'.
Coordinating conjunction linking alternatives: 'Kino oder Theater?' Normal word order (no inversion) with 'oder'.
Farewell phrase used when you have an arrangement and will see the person at the agreed time. Casual register.
Short positive response to a suggestion. Can also say 'Das ist eine gute Idee.' The shortened form drops 'Das ist eine'.

vocabulary.english: I'm not in the mood. / I don't feel like it.
vocabulary.usage_note: Short refusal phrase. Full form: 'Ich habe keine Lust.' Casual spoken German. Use when declining an invitation politely but casually.
Apology phrase. Literal meaning: 'does sorrow to me'. Used to express regret or apologize: 'Tut mir leid, ich habe keine Zeit.'
Informal agreement or acknowledgment. Borrowed from English. Used widely in spoken A1 German: 'Okay, bis dann!'
Question word for time. Introduces time questions: 'Wann beginnt der Film?' Answer always involves a time expression (am Montag, um drei Uhr, etc.).

examples.german: Heute bin ich frei — gehen wir ins Museum!
examples.english: Today I am free — let's go to the museum!
examples.note: Uses: heute (adverb); das Museum, frei from Lektion 8.

examples.german: Der Film beginnt um halb acht — wir müssen jetzt gehen.
examples.english: The film starts at half past seven — we have to go now.
examples.note: Uses: um (preposition for time), halb (half past = 30 min before next hour). halb acht = 7:30.

examples.german: Wann beginnt das Konzert? — Um Viertel nach sieben.
examples.english: When does the concert start? — At quarter past seven.
examples.note: Uses: Wann? (question adverb), um + Viertel nach (time expression).

examples.german: Am Abend gehen wir in die Bar oder ins Restaurant.
examples.english: In the evening we go to the bar or the restaurant.
examples.note: Uses: am (preposition with time of day), in (direction), oder (conjunction).

examples.german: Tut mir leid, heute Abend habe ich keine Lust ins Kino zu gehen.
examples.english: I am sorry, I don't feel like going to the cinema this evening.
examples.note: Uses: tut mir leid (phrase), heute (adverb), Keine Lust (phrase); das Kino from Lektion 8.

examples.german: Es ist Viertel vor neun — gehen wir! Bis dann!
examples.english: It is quarter to nine — let's go! See you then!
examples.note: Uses: vor (in time expression Viertel vor), Bis dann! (farewell phrase).

examples.german: Gute Idee! Wir treffen uns um halb drei vor dem Café.
examples.english: Good idea! We'll meet at half past two in front of the café.
examples.note: Uses: Gute Idee! (phrase), um (time preposition), halb (half past), vor (before/in front of).

examples.german: Wann bist du frei? Am Donnerstag oder am Freitag?
examples.english: When are you free? On Thursday or on Friday?
examples.note: Uses: Wann? (question adverb), am (with days), oder (conjunction); der Donnerstag, der Freitag from Lektion 8.

exercises.prompt: ___ bin ich im Schwimmbad. (today)
exercises.explanation: 'heute' = today. Time adverb; placed at the beginning for emphasis, or after the verb: 'Ich bin heute im Schwimmbad.'

exercises.prompt: Der Film beginnt ___ 20 Uhr. (at — clock time)
exercises.explanation: 'um' is used before clock times: 'um 20 Uhr' = at 8 pm.

exercises.prompt: Wir gehen ___ Montag ins Kino. (on — with day)
exercises.explanation: 'am' (an + dem) is used before days of the week: am Montag = on Monday.

exercises.prompt: ___ beginnt das Theater? (when?)
exercises.explanation: 'Wann' = when? Question word for time. Used to ask when something happens.

exercises.prompt: Ins Café ___ in die Bar? (or)
exercises.explanation: 'oder' = or. Coordinating conjunction linking two alternatives.

exercises.prompt: Es ist Viertel ___ drei. (past — clock time)
exercises.explanation: 'nach' in clock times = past. 'Viertel nach drei' = quarter past three (3:15).

exercises.prompt: Es ist zehn ___ vier. (to — clock time)
exercises.explanation: 'vor' in clock times = to/before. 'zehn vor vier' = ten to four (3:50).

exercises.prompt: CAUTION: '___ acht' means 7:30, not 8:30! (half past seven in German)
exercises.explanation: 'halb' in German means 30 minutes BEFORE the stated hour. 'halb acht' = half before eight = 7:30. Spanish: son las siete y media.

exercises.prompt: ___ mir leid, aber ich bin am Mittwoch nicht frei. (I am sorry)
exercises.explanation: 'Tut mir leid' = I am sorry. Fixed phrase: tut (3rd person sing. of 'tun') + mir (dative 'me') + leid (sorrow).

exercises.prompt: Gehen wir ins Museum? — ___ Idee! (Good idea!)
exercises.explanation: 'Gute Idee!' — the adjective 'gut' gets an -e ending before the feminine noun 'Idee' (without article).

exercises.prompt: Ich gehe ___ die Disco. (into — movement)
exercises.explanation: 'in die Disco' (accusative: movement into a place). With feminine nouns, 'in' + accusative → 'in die'.

exercises.prompt: Ich habe ___ Lust. Lass uns ___ Hause bleiben. (no desire / at home — use 'keine' and 'zu')
exercises.explanation: 'Keine Lust' = no desire/not in the mood. 'Zu Hause bleiben' = to stay at home (zu Hause = fixed phrase).

exercises.prompt: ___ gehst du ins Schwimmbad — ___ Dienstag ___ Donnerstag? (When / on / or)
exercises.explanation: Wann (when?), am (with days of the week), oder (or). Three 'others' category words in one sentence.

exercises.prompt: Der Film beginnt ___ halb neun.
exercises.explanation: 'um' is used before clock times. 'am' is for days/parts of day. 'in' is directional.

exercises.prompt: Wir gehen ___ Freitag ins Restaurant.
exercises.explanation: 'am Freitag' = on Friday. 'am' (an + dem) before days of week. 'um' is for clock times.

exercises.prompt: '___ halb zehn' means ___.
exercises.explanation: 'halb zehn' = half BEFORE ten = 9:30. German 'halb' counts back 30 minutes from the next full hour.

exercises.prompt: ___ leid, ich kann heute nicht kommen.
exercises.explanation: 'Tut mir leid' is the fixed expression for 'I am sorry'. 'Macht mir leid' and 'Gibt mir leid' do not exist.

exercises.prompt: Es ist Viertel ___ acht (7:45).
exercises.explanation: 'Viertel vor acht' = quarter to eight = 7:45. 'vor' in time expressions = to/before.

exercises.prompt: Gehen wir ins Kino ___ ins Theater?
exercises.explanation: 'oder' = or — offering a choice between two options. 'und' = and; 'aber' = but.

exercises.prompt: Today I am going to the cinema.
exercises.explanation: 'heute' = today; placed first for emphasis → verb stays second (gehe); 'ins Kino' = in das Kino.

exercises.prompt: The museum opens at half past nine.
exercises.solution: Das Museum öffnet um halb zehn.
exercises.explanation: 'um halb zehn' = at 9:30 (half before ten). Note: 'um' before time expressions.

exercises.prompt: When is the café open?
exercises.explanation: 'Wann' = when? Question word at the start; verb second: ist; das Café (neut.).

exercises.prompt: I am sorry, I'm not in the mood.
exercises.solution: Tut mir leid, ich habe keine Lust.
exercises.explanation: 'Tut mir leid' + 'ich habe keine Lust'. Two Lektion 8 phrases combined.

exercises.prompt: When are you free — on Thursday or on Friday?
exercises.solution: Wann bist du frei — am Donnerstag oder am Freitag?
exercises.explanation: Wann (question adverb); am + day; oder (conjunction). Classic Lektion 8 scheduling question.

exercises.prompt: Good idea! Let's go to the disco at quarter past eight.
exercises.solution: Gute Idee! Lass uns um Viertel nach acht in die Disco gehen.
exercises.explanation: 'Gute Idee!'; 'um Viertel nach acht' = at 8:15; 'in die Disco' (accusative, movement).

exercises.prompt: See you then! I'll be there at half past seven.
exercises.solution: Bis dann! Ich bin um halb acht da.
exercises.explanation: 'Bis dann!' = farewell phrase; 'um halb acht' = at 7:30; 'da sein' = to be there.

exercises.prompt: Der Film beginnt am 19 Uhr.
exercises.explanation: Clock times use 'um': 'um 19 Uhr'. 'am' is used with days of the week and parts of the day, not clock times.

exercises.prompt: Wir gehen um Freitag ins Restaurant.
exercises.solution: Wir gehen am Freitag ins Restaurant.
exercises.explanation: 'am Freitag' = on Friday. 'am' (an + dem) is used with days. 'um' is only for clock times.

exercises.prompt: 'Halb zehn' ist 10:30 Uhr. (correct this misconception)
exercises.explanation: 'halb zehn' = half BEFORE ten = 9:30. This is the most common error for English/Spanish speakers.

exercises.prompt: Tut mir leid, aber heute Abend habe ich kein Lust.
exercises.solution: Tut mir leid, aber heute Abend habe ich keine Lust.
exercises.explanation: 'keine Lust' — die Lust is feminine, so 'keine' (not 'kein'). Always: keine Lust.

exercises.prompt: Es ist zehn nach vor acht.
exercises.explanation: Use either 'nach' (past) or 'vor' (to/before) but not both together. 'zehn vor acht' = ten to eight (7:50).

exercises.prompt: Gut Idee! Wann gehen wir in die Disco?
exercises.solution: Gute Idee! Wann gehen wir in die Disco?
exercises.explanation: 'Gute Idee!' — the adjective needs an -e ending before the feminine noun 'Idee' (strong inflection without article).

exercises.prompt: Wann gehst du ins Kino — am Donnerstag und am Freitag?
exercises.solution: Wann gehst du ins Kino — am Donnerstag oder am Freitag?
exercises.explanation: The question offers a choice (this day OR that day), so 'oder' (or) is correct, not 'und' (and).

exercises.prompt: Es ist ___ — das Café schließt ___ 22 Uhr. Gehen wir! (too late / at)
exercises.explanation: 'zu spät' = too late; 'um 22 Uhr' = at 10 pm. 'um' is always used with clock times.

exercises.prompt: On Monday evening I go to the bar.
exercises.solution: Am Montagabend gehe ich in die Bar.
exercises.explanation: 'am Montagabend' = on Monday evening (compound); 'in die Bar' (accusative, feminine).

exercises.prompt: Ich weiß nicht, ___ das Theater öffnet — ___ 19 Uhr ___ 20 Uhr? (when / at / or)
exercises.explanation: Wann (question adverb in embedded question); um (before clock time); oder (giving alternatives).

exercises.prompt: 'Viertel nach sechs' ist ___.
exercises.explanation: 'Viertel nach sechs' = quarter past six = 6:15. 'nach' = past.

exercises.prompt: 'Viertel vor sieben' ist ___.
exercises.explanation: 'Viertel vor sieben' = quarter to seven = 6:45. 'vor' = to/before.

exercises.prompt: Sorry, today I'm not in the mood for the cinema.
exercises.solution: Tut mir leid, heute habe ich keine Lust auf das Kino.
exercises.explanation: 'Tut mir leid' (apology phrase); 'heute' (today); 'keine Lust auf + accusative' = not in the mood for.

exercises.prompt: Okay, bis ___! (See you then!)
exercises.explanation: 'Bis dann!' = See you then! A farewell phrase used after making plans.

exercises.prompt: Wann beginnt der Film? — Um am sieben Uhr.
exercises.solution: Wann beginnt der Film? — Um sieben Uhr.
exercises.explanation: Use 'um' OR 'am' — not both together. For clock times: 'um sieben Uhr'. 'am' is for days/parts of day.

exercises.prompt: It is ten past three. (3:10)
exercises.explanation: 'zehn nach drei' = ten past three = 3:10. Pattern: [minutes] nach [hour].

exercises.prompt: It is five to four. (3:55)
exercises.explanation: 'fünf vor vier' = five to four = 3:55. Pattern: [minutes] vor [next hour].

exercises.prompt: ___ du ___ Museum, ins Kino ___ ins Theater gehen? (when / into the / or)
exercises.explanation: Wann (when); ins (in + das — neuter accusative contraction); oder (or — giving multiple alternatives).

exercises.prompt: Tut mir leid — heute bin ich keine frei, ich habe einen Termin.
exercises.solution: Tut mir leid — heute bin ich nicht frei, ich habe einen Termin.
exercises.explanation: 'nicht frei' = not free/not available. 'keine' negates nouns; 'nicht' negates adjectives and verbs.
